Also: 5 research-led projects — systems thinking, field research & end-to-end ownership from zero

HOW I THINK

The thinking behind
every decision.

01

Systems before screens

At Qubo, adding a new device category is not just a design problem; it is a mental model problem. I map the existing system before touching a single screen.

Audit Mapping Scaling
02

Research that de-risks

For Sense 2.0, primary research in Bangalore markets came before designing a single screen: 10+ store personas and the AODR framework turned ambiguity into defensible architecture.

Modeling Alignment
03

Edge cases are the design

Architecture taught me that the middle is where people live. I design for failure states first because the edge case reveals what the system actually is.

Edge cases Validation Handover

ABOUT

A design thinker
who also ships.

I started with architecture: learning to hold a whole system before touching a detail. NID gave me Universal Design as an ethical frame, designing for the full range of humans, not the median. IoT gave me the constraint of hardware reality.

Most researchers cannot ship. Most designers do not research. I do both, and the rarest work happens at that intersection.

Let’s work on
something hard.

Research-heavy roles, design strategy, and product companies building genuinely complex things. IoT, health tech, fintech; let’s talk.
